examples.sentence: 彼の歌声は聞くに堪える。
examples.translation: His singing voice is worth listening to.
examples.translationEn: His singing voice is worth listening to.
examples.romaji: Kare no utagoe wa kiku ni taeru.

examples.sentence: この小説は読むに堪える内容だ。
examples.translation: This novel is worth reading.
examples.translationEn: This novel is worth reading.
examples.romaji: Kono shousetsu wa yomu ni taeru naiyou da.

examples.sentence: あの映画は見るに堪える名作だ。
examples.translation: That movie is a masterpiece worth watching.
examples.translationEn: That movie is a masterpiece worth watching.
examples.romaji: Ano eiga wa miru ni taeru meisaku da.

examples.sentence: 彼の意見は十分に参考に堪える。
examples.translation: His opinion is definitely worth considering.
examples.translationEn: His opinion is definitely worth considering.
examples.romaji: Kare no iken wa juubun ni sankou ni taeru.

examples.sentence: 彼は厳しい修行に耐えた。
examples.translation: He endured the harsh training.
examples.translationEn: He endured the harsh training.
examples.romaji: Kare wa kibishii shugyou ni taeta.

examples.sentence: この橋は強風に耐えられる。
examples.translation: This bridge can withstand strong winds.
examples.translationEn: This bridge can withstand strong winds.
examples.romaji: Kono hashi wa kyoufuu ni taerareru.

examples.sentence: 彼女は苦しみに耐えた。
examples.translation: She endured the pain.
examples.translationEn: She endured the pain.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kurushimi ni taeta.

examples.sentence: この建物は地震に耐えることができます。
examples.translation: This building can withstand an earthquake.
examples.translationEn: This building can withstand an earthquake.
examples.romaji: Kono tatemono wa jishin ni taeru koto ga dekimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼は練習を重ねて、ついにプロのギタリストに至った。
examples.translation: After repeated practice, he finally became a professional guitarist.
examples.translationEn: After repeated practice, he finally became a professional guitarist.
examples.romaji: Kare wa renshuu o kasanete, tsui ni puro no gitarisuto ni itatta.

examples.sentence: 調査を進めるうちに、驚くべき結論に至った。
examples.translation: As the investigation progressed, it led to a surprising conclusion.
examples.translationEn: As the investigation progressed, it led to a surprising conclusion.
examples.romaji: Chousa o susumeru uchi ni, odoroku beki ketsuron ni itatta.

examples.sentence: この問題はついに国際的な対立に至った。
examples.translation: This issue finally led to an international conflict.
examples.translationEn: This issue finally led to an international conflict.
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa tsui ni kokusaiteki na tairitsu ni itatta.

examples.sentence: たくさんの失敗を経て、成功に至った。
examples.translation: After many failures, I finally achieved success.
examples.translationEn: After many failures, I finally achieved success.
examples.romaji: Takusan no shippai o hete, seikou ni itatta.

examples.sentence: 早く準備するに越したことはない。
examples.translation: It's best to prepare early.
examples.translationEn: It's best to prepare early.
examples.romaji: Hayaku junbi suru ni koshita koto wa nai.

examples.sentence: 新鮮な食材を使うに越したことはない。
examples.translation: There's nothing better than using fresh ingredients.
examples.translationEn: There's nothing better than using fresh ingredients.
examples.romaji: Shinsen na shokuzai o tsukau ni koshita koto wa nai.

examples.sentence: 安全運転に越したことはない。
examples.translation: Safe driving is always best.
examples.translationEn: Safe driving is always best.
examples.romaji: Anzen unten ni koshita koto wa nai.

examples.sentence: 健康的な生活を送るに越したことはない。
examples.translation: There's nothing better than leading a healthy lifestyle.
examples.translationEn: There's nothing better than leading a healthy lifestyle.
examples.romaji: Kenkouteki na seikatsu o okuru ni koshita koto wa nai.

examples.sentence: 彼の喜びは想像に難くない。
examples.translation: It's not hard to imagine his delight.
examples.translationEn: It's not hard to imagine his delight.
examples.romaji: Kare no yorokobi wa souzou ni katakunai.

examples.sentence: この結果が彼女を失望させたことは理解に難くない。
examples.translation: It's not difficult to understand that this outcome disappointed her.
examples.translationEn: It's not difficult to understand that this outcome disappointed her.
examples.romaji: Kono kekka ga kanojo o shitsubou saseta koto wa rikai ni katakunai.

examples.sentence: 彼が怒る気持ちは察するに難くない。
examples.translation: It's not hard to guess why he is angry.
examples.translationEn: It's not hard to guess why he is angry.
examples.romaji: Kare ga okoru kimochi wa sassuru ni katakunai.

examples.sentence: このような状況が起こった理由は想像に難くない。
examples.translation: It's not hard to imagine why this situation occurred.
examples.translationEn: It's not hard to imagine why this situation occurred.
examples.romaji: Kono youna joukyou ga okotta riyuu wa souzou ni katakunai.

examples.grammarPoint: ～のは Noun ぐらいのものだ
examples.sentence: この小説を読むのは暇つぶしぐらいのものだ。
examples.translation: Reading this novel is nothing but a way to kill time.
examples.translationEn: Reading this novel is nothing but a way to kill time.
examples.romaji: Kono shousetsu o yomu no wa hima tsubushi gurai no mono da.

examples.grammarPoint: ～のは Noun ぐらいのものだ
examples.sentence: 今日の夕食で良かったのはピザぐらいのものだ。
examples.translation: About the only good thing at tonight's dinner was the pizza.
examples.translationEn: About the only good thing at tonight's dinner was the pizza.
examples.romaji: Kyou no yuushoku de yokatta no wa piza gurai no mono da.

examples.grammarPoint: ～のは Noun ぐらいのものだ
examples.sentence: この映画を見るのは時間の無駄ぐらいのものだ。
examples.translation: Watching this movie is nothing but a waste of time.
examples.translationEn: Watching this movie is nothing but a waste of time.
examples.romaji: Kono eiga o miru no wa jikan no muda gurai no mono da.

examples.grammarPoint: ～のは Noun ぐらいのものだ
examples.sentence: 彼と話すのはストレスぐらいのものだ。
examples.translation: Talking to him is nothing but stressful.
examples.translationEn: Talking to him is nothing but stressful.
examples.romaji: Kare to hanasu no wa sutoresu gurai no mono da.

examples.sentence: 早く起きれば、遅刻しなかったものを。
examples.translation: If only I'd woken up early, I wouldn't have been late.
examples.translationEn: If only I'd woken up early, I wouldn't have been late.
examples.romaji: Hayaku okireba, chikoku shinakatta mono o.

examples.sentence: もっと頑張れば、テストに合格したものを。
examples.translation: If only I'd tried harder, I would have passed the test.
examples.translationEn: If only I'd tried harder, I would have passed the test.
examples.romaji: Motto ganbareba, tesuto ni goukaku shita mono o.

examples.sentence: お金があれば、新しい車を買ったものを。
examples.translation: If only I had the money, I would have bought a new car.
examples.translationEn: If only I had the money, I would have bought a new car.
examples.romaji: Okane ga areba, atarashii kuruma o katta mono o.

examples.sentence: 友達がいれば、一緒に遊んだものを。
examples.translation: If only I had a friend, I would have hung out together.
examples.translationEn: If only I had a friend, I would have hung out together.
examples.romaji: Tomodachi ga ireba, issho ni asonda mono o.

examples.sentence: 大人びた考え方をする子供
examples.translation: A child who thinks in a very grown-up way
examples.translationEn: A child who thinks in a very grown-up way
examples.romaji: Otonabita kangaekata o suru kodomo

examples.sentence: 古びたカバンを捨てられない。
examples.translation: I can't throw away that old, worn-looking bag.
examples.translationEn: I can't throw away that old, worn-looking bag.
examples.romaji: Furubita kaban o suterarenai.

examples.sentence: 田舎びた雰囲気がこの町の魅力だ。
examples.translation: The countrified atmosphere is this town’s charm.
examples.translationEn: The countrified atmosphere is this town’s charm.
examples.romaji: Inakabita fun'iki ga kono machi no miryoku da.

examples.sentence: 彼女は少し大人びた服装をしている。
examples.translation: She’s dressed in a slightly grown-up style.
examples.translationEn: She’s dressed in a slightly grown-up style.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa sukoshi otonabita fukusou o shite iru.

examples.sentence: 彼女は年齢より大人びている。
examples.translation: She seems older than her actual age.
examples.translationEn: She seems older than her actual age.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa nenrei yori otona bite iru.

examples.sentence: この建物はすっかり古びている。
examples.translation: This building has become completely old and worn.
examples.translationEn: This building has become completely old and worn.
examples.romaji: Kono tatemono wa sukkari furubite iru.

examples.sentence: 彼は都会育ちなのに、少し田舎びている話し方をする。
examples.translation: Even though he grew up in the city, he speaks in a somewhat country-like way.
examples.translationEn: Even though he grew up in the city, he speaks in a somewhat country-like way.
examples.romaji: Kare wa tokai sodachi na noni, sukoshi inakabite iru hanashikata o suru.

examples.sentence: 子供が急に大人びる瞬間を見ると驚く。
examples.translation: It’s surprising when you see a child suddenly act grown-up.
examples.translationEn: It’s surprising when you see a child suddenly act grown-up.
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga kyuu ni otona biru shunkan o miru to odoroku.

examples.sentence: 彼は何でも知っているかのようにぶった態度を取る。
examples.translation: He takes on an attitude as though he knows everything.
examples.translationEn: He takes on an attitude as though he knows everything.
examples.romaji: Kare wa nandemo shitteiru ka no you ni butta taido o toru.

examples.sentence: 偉そうぶった物言いをされると、少し腹が立つ。
examples.translation: It irritates me when he speaks in such a high-and-mighty way.
examples.translationEn: It irritates me when he speaks in such a high-and-mighty way.
examples.romaji: Erasou butta monoii o sareru to, sukoshi hara ga tatsu.

examples.sentence: 彼女は大人ぶった口調で話しているが、実はまだ高校生だ。
examples.translation: She’s speaking in a very grown-up manner, but she’s actually still in high school.
examples.translationEn: She’s speaking in a very grown-up manner, but she’s actually still in high school.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa otona butta kuchou de hanashite iru ga, jitsu wa mada koukousei da.

examples.sentence: いい子ぶった態度を取らなくてもいいよ。
examples.translation: You don't have to pretend to be such a goody-goody.
examples.translationEn: You don't have to pretend to be such a goody-goody.
examples.romaji: Ii ko butta taido o toranakute mo ii yo.
